publications.title: Structural insights into protection against a SARS-CoV-2 spike variant by T cell receptor diversity.
publications.authors: Wu D, Efimov GA, Bogolyubova AV, Pierce BG, Mariuzza RA.
publications.executive_summary: This study examined structurally diverse TCRs specific for an HLA-A2-restricted SARS-CoV-2 spike protein epitope (RLQ) to understand how T cell populations maintain recognition despite viral escape mutations. Two TCRs, RLQ3 and RLQ7, showed opposite responses to a common natural variant (T1006I): RLQ3 lost recognition while RLQ7 maintained or improved binding. Crystal structures of RLQ7 bound to both wild-type RLQ and T1006I-HLA-A2 complexes revealed multiple distinct structural mechanisms by which different TCRs can recognize the same epitope, explaining how TCR clonal diversity at this epitope ensures robust protection against viral escape and prevents loss of T cell immunity from single mutations.
